title: HUG-25: Sticky Action Bar for Unsaved Time Changes
description: Replace the current 'Spara ändringar' button on the time entry page with a sticky action bar that appears only when there are unsaved changes. The action bar provides clear feedback about unsaved changes and offers Cancel/Save actions, improving user awareness and preventing accidental data loss.

userStories.title: Create sticky action bar component
userStories.description:
Create a reusable `UnsavedChangesBar.tsx` component in `apps/web/src/components/`.

Component interface:
```tsx
interface UnsavedChangesBarProps {
  visible: boolean
  onCancel: () => void  // Hides bar, keeps changes
  onSave: () => void
  isSaving: boolean
}
```

Requirements:
1. Desktop (≥768px): Fixed bottom bar, full width, elevated with shadow, semi-transparent background.
2. Mobile (<768px): Overlay/modal style with backdrop blur or semi-transparent background, slightly larger buttons.
3. Contains text: "Du har osparade ändringar - ändringar måste sparas"
4. Contains two buttons: "Avbryt" (outline/secondary) and "Spara" (primary). "Spara" shows "Sparar..." when `isSaving` is true.
5. Desktop layout: Warning text on left side, buttons on right side.
6. Mobile layout: Stack vertically (text above buttons).
7. Use yellow/amber warning color scheme (bg-amber-50, border-amber-300).
8. Smooth slide-in animation from bottom with ease-out timing (300ms duration):
   ```tsx
   className={cn(
     'fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 transition-transform duration-300 ease-out',
     visible ? 'translate-y-0' : 'translate-y-full'
   )}
   ```
9. Smooth slide-out animation when hiding.
10. z-index ensures it appears above other content.
11. Use existing Button component for consistency.
12. Appropriate padding and spacing (p-4 or similar).

Mockup reference: https://pasteboard.co/3C6D1UJr7dSx.png

Run typecheck to confirm no errors. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Show action bar after field blur on time changes
userStories.description:
Integrate the UnsavedChangesBar into the time entry page so it appears after the user finishes editing a time field.

In `apps/web/src/pages/TimePage.tsx`:

1. Add a separate `showActionBar` state to control visibility independently from `hasChanges`.
2. Trigger `showActionBar = true` on `onBlur` of time input fields when `hasChanges` is true.
3. Action bar does NOT appear immediately on first keystroke - only after blur.
4. Action bar stays visible as long as `hasChanges` is true and `showActionBar` is true.
5. Action bar hides when `hasChanges` becomes false (e.g., after save).
6. Only hours/minutes fields count as "changes" (not project or other fields).
7. Works on both mobile and desktop views.

Run typecheck to confirm no errors. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Implement Cancel action to hide bar (keep changes)
userStories.description:
Implement the Cancel ("Avbryt") button behavior in the action bar.

In `apps/web/src/pages/TimePage.tsx`:

1. "Avbryt" button sets `showActionBar = false` immediately.
2. Unsaved changes are PRESERVED (not discarded) - `hasChanges` state remains `true`.
3. Time input fields retain their edited values.
4. Action bar slides out smoothly (the component animation handles this).
5. Action bar can reappear later on next blur of a modified time input field.

Run typecheck to confirm no errors. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Block navigation with unsaved changes warning
Remove old save button and integrate action bar
userStories.description:
Replace the old save button with the new UnsavedChangesBar component.

In `apps/web/src/pages/TimePage.tsx` (lines 474-481):

1. Remove the existing "Spara ändringar" button.
2. Add the `UnsavedChangesBar` component with props:
   ```tsx
   <UnsavedChangesBar
     visible={showActionBar && hasChanges}
     onCancel={() => setShowActionBar(false)}
     onSave={handleSave}
     isSaving={isSaving}
   />
   ```
3. No visual layout shift when removing old button.
4. Ensure save action sets both `hasChanges` and `showActionBar` to false on success.

Run typecheck to confirm no errors. Verify in browser using dev-browser skill.

userStories.title: Style action bar according to mockup and test responsiveness
userStories.description:
Ensure the action bar matches the mockup design and works at all screen sizes.

Mockup reference: https://pasteboard.co/3C6D1UJr7dSx.png

Verify the following using dev-browser skill at multiple viewports:

1. 320px width (small mobile): Overlay style, text stacked above buttons, no overflow.
2. 375px width (standard mobile): Same as above, buttons are slightly larger.
3. 768px width (tablet): Fixed bottom bar, text on left, buttons on right.
4. 1024px+ (desktop): Full-width bar, shadow elevation, semi-transparent background.

At each breakpoint confirm:
- Amber/yellow warning color scheme is applied (bg-amber-50, border-amber-300).
- "Avbryt" uses outline/secondary variant.
- "Spara" uses primary variant.
- Slide-in animation from bottom uses ease-out timing (300ms).
- Bar appears above all other content (proper z-index).
- Clear visual separation from main content (shadow/border).
- All touch targets are at least 44x44px on mobile.
- Layout feels natural and uncluttered.

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill on multiple screen sizes.
